title: Quiz: Phase Gates
description: Test your understanding of S, T, and general phase gates

questions.question: What is S|1⟩, where S is the S gate?
questions.explanation: The S gate applies a π/2 phase: S = [[1,0],[0,i]]. So S|1⟩ = i|1⟩.

questions.question: What is the relationship between S and Z gates?
questions.explanation: S² = Z. The S gate is the square root of Z, applying half the phase rotation.

questions.question: What is T², where T is the T gate?
questions.explanation: T² = S. The T gate applies π/4 phase, so T² applies π/2 phase, which is the S gate.

questions.question: Phase gates change the measurement probabilities in the computational basis.
questions.explanation: False! Phase gates only change the relative phase. For state α|0⟩ + β|1⟩, applying P(θ) gives α|0⟩ + e^(iθ)β|1⟩. Since |e^(iθ)β|² = |β|², probabilities remain unchanged.

questions.question: What is T⁸?
questions.explanation: T⁸ = I. Since T = P(π/4), T⁸ = P(2π) = I. The phase completes a full rotation.

questions.question: The general phase gate P(θ) has the matrix form:
questions.explanation: P(θ) = [[1, 0], [0, e^(iθ)]]. It leaves |0⟩ unchanged and multiplies |1⟩ by e^(iθ).

questions.question: On the Bloch sphere, what transformation does the S gate perform?
questions.explanation: The S gate performs a 90° (π/2) rotation around the Z-axis of the Bloch sphere.
